name: Saint Lucia Buns
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the saffron in water and set aside.
2. Heat the milk to lukewarm (115°F) and stir in the yeast. Let sit for 10 minutes.
3. Add saffron mixture, butter, sugar, eggs, and salt to the flour. Gradually beat until well combined but stiff.
4. Turn dough into a greased bowl, coating all sides. Cover with plastic wrap. Refrigerate for at least two hours or up to 2 days.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F.
6. Turn dough out onto a floured board and knead briefly to warm and soften it.
7. Cut the dough into twice as many pieces as desired buns (16 buns = 32 pieces).
8. Shape each piece into a 7-inch long log.
9. Curl both ends of each log in the same direction.
10. Place one raisin in the center of each curl and pinch two logs together back to back for each bun, forming Lussekatter.
11. Place the finished rolls on greased baking sheets.
12. Cover and let rise in a warm place until puffed (about 45 minutes).
13. Brush buns with sugar.
14. Bake until just golden brown (10 minutes).
15. Transfer to wire racks to cool slightly. Serve warm with butter and hot coffee.
